| ~~065~~ | 2026-06-08 LOOM cycle-8 D-A2S1B2 live-smoke | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-08 (LOOM-52, Cycle 10).** Root cause was NOT worker-ai. The M1 migration ([knowledge-service/app/db/migrate.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/db/migrate.py)) silently renamed the `extraction_jobs_status_check` constraint `complete`→`completed` AND dropped `paused`/`cancelled` (its comment only claimed "add summarizing"), while the WHOLE codebase + FE emit `complete`/`paused`/`cancelled` (knowledge repo `update_status(...,"complete")`, `state_machine.JobStatus`, worker-ai `_complete_job`, `ExtractionJobsTab`). So every finished/paused/cancelled extraction CheckViolated → misreported `failed`. **Fix = reconcile the constraint** (inline L287 + M1 ALTER) to the full vocab `('pending','running','paused','summarizing','complete','failed','cancelled')` — a widening; **NO worker-ai/state_machine/FE change** (the roadmap's "fix worker-ai complete→completed" was the wrong diagnosis — it would diverge from the repo+FE and still leave paused/cancelled broken). Regression-lock: real-PG integration test (all 7 statuses accepted, `test_extraction_jobs_status_check_accepts_full_vocabulary`) + DDL string tests. /review-impl: 2 LOW + 1 COSMETIC folded (summarizing reserved-state doc, EXCEPTION-swallow caveat, test rename); cleared the cross-service replica concern (translation-service `extraction_jobs` is a separate DB/table with no status CHECK). Live dev `extraction_jobs` = 2 `failed` rows (the symptom), 0 `completed` → ADD will succeed on next knowledge-service restart. **Full worker-ai→knowledge extraction chain smoke → cycle 11 (066).** | DONE | — |
| 066 | 2026-06-08 LOOM cycle-8 D-A2S1B2 live-smoke | **D-A2S1B2-STATUS-EFFECTS-PRODUCER-GAP — diagnosis corrected + instrumented (LOOM-53, Cycle 11).** The cycle-8 hypothesis "`canonical_id=NULL` (anchoring) blocks the status" is **WRONG**: the Pass-2 status resolver ([pass2_writer.py](../../services/knowledge-service/app/extraction/pass2_writer.py) `_resolve_status_entity_id`) uses the chapter-local entity map keyed on `entity.id` (always set for any extracted entity — NO `≥2 links` requirement) → Tier-A.1 resolves a dead character that was extracted this chapter regardless of glossary anchoring. The whole producer path is **statically CORRECT** end-to-end: event-extraction prompt asks for `status_effects` (death→`gone`, example "Zhao"), the parser/`StatusEffect` model handles them, `event_order` is non-null in the full publish path, the resolver matches on `entity.id`. So cycle-8's "no `:EntityStatus`" = LLM non-compliance (didn't emit a well-formed status_effect) OR a path-specific event_order/fold miss — **the two silent-skip points are now a metric** (`knowledge_extraction_status_effect_total{outcome}`) so the cause is observable, not guessed. 066 is independent of 065 (status write precedes `_complete_job`). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-06-09 (LOOM-63 / FD-4) — the real bug was found + fixed.** The canonical 067 run (a part-less death-chapter book, gemma) showed entities persisted but 0 `:EntityStatus`, with knowledge logs `A2-S1b: status_effect skipped — event 'Bryn dies' has no event_order`. Root cause: `event_order` (= `chapter_base`) was derived **only** from `hierarchy_paths.chapter_index`, and the worker builds `hierarchy_paths` ONLY when `hierarchy.part is not None` → a **flat book (chapters, no parts)** got `event_order=None` → every `status_effect` skipped AND the dense timeline axis collapsed. Fix: thread the chapter's own `sort_order` as a separate `chapter_index` (pass2_writer fallback + persist-pass2 request field + worker client + runner capture-outside-part-gate). **Live-proven on the fixed/rebuilt stack:** part-less death-chapter → 2 fresh `:EntityStatus{gone}` with `from_order`=chapter_sort×stride (was 0). 5 regression tests. **Compounding cycle-8 cause also confirmed:** the original job used qwen3.6-35b (a reasoning model → emits empty/think → 0 status) + failed on the M1 constraint (065, cycle-10 fixed). | DONE | — |

| 069 | 2026-06-08 LOOM cycle-13-BUILD (was LOOM-43 LOW-1) | **D-A3-REPLACE-ORPHAN-ARC-NODES (BE).** Precisely diagnosed during the planner-FE build: `outline.commit_decomposed_tree` ([services/composition-service/app/db/repositories/outline.py](../../services/composition-service/app/db/repositories/outline.py)) ALWAYS inserts a NEW `arc` node + NEW `chapter` nodes each commit, and on `replace=true` soft-archives ONLY the target chapters' SCENES (not the prior arc/chapter nodes). So every re-plan leaves the PRIOR arc node + its chapter nodes childless (their scenes archived, a fresh arc/chapters created alongside) → orphan arc/chapter nodes accumulate in the outline. **Clean fix is BE, not FE** (the planner UI doesn't render the outline tree, so there's no FE display to filter): on `replace`, also soft-archive the prior arc + its now-childless chapter nodes for the target chapters (the recursive soft-archive idiom already exists — outline.py:5-6). Low urgency (archived scenes already hide; the orphan nodes are inert structure). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (LOOM-78 / FD-17).** On `replace`, `commit_decomposed_tree` now archives `scene`+`chapter` nodes for all `affected` chapter_ids (also reaps pre-accumulated orphan chapter nodes) + a SCOPED childless-arc sweep over only the parent arcs of the archived chapters (`NOT EXISTS` active chapter child). **/review-impl HIGH:** the initial sweep was project-wide → would silently archive an unrelated freshly-created empty arc; re-scoped to the affected arcs (bystander regression test added). 3 real-PG tests (full-replace · partial-replace preserves out-of-target arc · bystander survives); 47 integration pass. | composition-service (outline repo) | DONE |

| 053 | 2026-05-31 Phase-B BUILD subB adversary F1 (WARN) | **D-PHASE-B-EMIT-PERMANENT-FAILURE-SURFACE.** `emit_correction`'s blanket `except Exception` (correct for transient PG faults — best-effort §6.6) also swallows PERMANENT failures (malformed payload, serialization edge, future schema drift) with only a `logger.warning`. The §10.1 replay backstop can only replay rows that made it INTO `outbox_events` — a row whose INSERT never succeeded has zero durability. Fix: distinguish transient (pool/conn) from permanent failures; surface non-transient via a metric / error-log-at-ERROR rather than silent drop. Low risk today (payload shape is code-controlled, json.dumps(default=str)). **Empirically confirmed (session 75 smoke):** a non-UUID-coercible `aggregate_id` (a synthetic relation id) made `emit_correction` silently drop the correction (uuid.UUID() raised → swallowed). Harmless in production (all real entity/relation/event ids are 32-hex → UUID-coercible), but it is a concrete instance of the swallow-masks-permanent-failure concern. | Phase E hardening (when corrections feed gold) | LOW |

### Lore-enrichment workstream deferrals (`LE-` prefix; merged 2026-06-01)
> Carried under `LE-` to avoid the numeric-ID collision with main's Phase-B/geo track (both branches independently allocated 044–055). | ~~LE-056~~ | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C15 WARN-3 | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-01 (C2, post-main-merge re-decision).** Judge-diversity floor: `app/eval/judge_usefulness.py` `acceptable` now requires ≥2 judges from **≥2 DISTINCT families** (`JudgeSpec.family`, default=label, normalized strip+casefold so case/whitespace can't fake diversity) **AND** κ ≥ a configurable floor (`settings.judge_kappa_floor` / `LORE_ENRICHMENT_JUDGE_KAPPA_FLOOR`, default 0.0 = below-chance disqualifies; non-finite κ treated as uncomputable). | ~~LE-051~~ | 2026-05-30 lore-enrichment C14-FIX (WARN-1) | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-04 (verified token-safe; cursor = won't-fix micro-opt).** Re-reviewed `runner.run_job`: a gap in `skip_gap_refs` is skipped at runner.py:198 **BEFORE** the cost-charge AND the LLM `run_gap` (`outcome.resumed_skipped.append(...); continue`) — so a resumed job spends **neither budget nor tokens** on done gaps (the real concern). The resume worker passes ALL already-persisted `gap_ref`s, so done gaps never reach the reload/`deduped` path. The only thing NOT done is persisting a "remainder cursor" so the runner doesn't iterate-and-skip the done prefix — but that skip is pure in-memory iteration (no I/O, no LLM), negligible even for a 1000-gap job. **Won't-fix:** the micro-opt changes no correctness or cost and adds state-tracking complexity. Token-safety + convergence are proven (test pins partial-resume). | DONE (token-safe; cursor won't-fix) | RESOLVED |

| LE-059 | 2026-06-01 lore-enrichment C1 token metering | **P2/P3 post-call token reconcile + real embed usage.** ✅ **(a) DONE 2026-06-01:** `assembly.build_live_runner` now wires the `UsageMeter` for ALL techniques (was P1-only) — P2 fabrication / P3 re-cook pre-charge in tokens (3000/4500) and use the same metered seams, so a multi-pass gap's REAL token spend is captured (`UsageMeter` accumulates every call) + reconciled per gap, like P1; runner reconcile is technique-agnostic (test pins a fabrication-magnitude gap reconciling to real tokens). **(b) ✅ DONE 2026-06-04:** provider-registry `parseOpenAIEmbeddingResponse` now surfaces the upstream `usage.prompt_tokens` on `EmbedResult.PromptTokens` (json `prompt_tokens`, omitempty → wire-compatible; +2 Go tests); the `/internal/embed` handler returns `result` directly so it flows automatically. lore-enrichment `EmbedResult.prompt_tokens` + the embed seam meters on the REAL count when present, **falling back to the char-estimate only when the provider reports none** (Ollama / a too-old provider-registry) → no regression, never metered as 0 (+2 py tests: real-count vs fallback). **ADDITIVE + backward-compatible** (Go/Python both ignore unknown fields). **Op:** takes effect live only after a provider-registry rebuild (shared daemon — clobber-prone per LE-061; until then lore-enrichment gracefully estimates). **✅ LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-06-04 (D-LE059B):** rebuilt+recreated provider-registry; `/internal/embed` (test-user bge-m3) → 200, dim 1024, embeddings present; the parse reads LM Studio's `usage` block but **LM Studio reports `prompt_tokens: 0` for embeddings** (confirmed by hitting `:1234/v1/embeddings` directly) → `omitempty` drops it → lore-enrichment falls back to the char-estimate. So the full chain works as designed; the REAL-count path is exercised only by providers that report non-zero embed usage (e.g. real OpenAI) — the Go unit test pins that parse. No regression on the local stack. /review-impl added a `>0` guard so a negative count can never reduce metered spend. **(c) ✅ CLOSED 2026-06-04 (won't-fix until parallelized).** Re-reviewed `runner.run_job`: gaps are processed in a strictly SEQUENTIAL `for gap in gaps` loop, and `_reconcile_gap(before_tokens)` snapshots `meter.total_tokens` immediately after the pre-charge and diffs after the gap runs — correct by construction while runs are sequential (the docstring pins the "sequential-run invariant"). There is nothing to change today; parallelizing gaps is not planned. If/when gaps are ever parallelized, the per-gap delta must move to a per-gap meter scope — but adding that machinery now would be speculative complexity. Closed as won't-fix-until-needed. | DONE (won't-fix until gaps parallelized) | RESOLVED |
| ~~LE-060~~ | 2026-06-01 lore-enrichment C3 auto-reject (F-C12-1 + /review-impl) | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-01.** `app/verify/canon_lookup.py extract_canon_terms` now segments CJK canon with **jieba POS-tagging** (`jieba>=0.42`, requirements.txt; lazily imported so its dict loads only when a canon term is actually extracted), keeping **proper-noun tags only** (ns/nr/nt/nz…) and dropping generic nouns/verbs/adjectives — consistent with the Latin Capitalized rule, so the C3 false-positive-safety holds (a benign common-word + negation can't fire a contradiction). | ~~LE-061~~ | 2026-06-02 live-e2e root-cause | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-02** — freshness guard hardened: added a provider-registry `/internal/embed` route-presence probe (catches a fully-stale binary missing the route) AND a **UNSTAMPED** warning when a first-party image has no `git_sha` label (surfaces the degraded tier-1-only detection that let this slip); `scripts/check_stack_freshness.py` + `drift_note()` + 2 new unit tests. provider-registry itself was rebuilt + SHA-stamped. ORIGINAL: **provider-registry was a STALE, unstamped image** (`org.loreweave.git_sha` label empty → never built via `build-stack.sh`); its binary predated the embed `/v1`-normalization (`internal/provider/embed.go` `TrimSuffix(base,"/v1")`). | ~~LE-062~~ | 2026-06-02 live-e2e ops | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-02** — replaced the inherited HTTP `/health` probe (meaningless for a headless consumer) with a **background-heartbeat liveness**: `app/worker/heartbeat.py` (a bg asyncio task touches a heartbeat file every 5s — stays fresh during long generations since the event loop is alive, goes stale only on a real hang/crash) + `app/worker/healthcheck.py` (`python -m app.worker.healthcheck` exits 0/1) + compose `healthcheck` override on the worker block + 7 unit tests. Wired in `app/worker/__main__.py` (both the active and consumer-disabled branches). ORIGINAL: **`lore-enrichment-worker` healthcheck is a permanent false-negative.** The compose `lore-enrichment-worker` block inherits the service's HTTP healthcheck (`urlopen http://127.0.0.1:8093/health`) but the worker is a headless Redis-stream consumer (`python -m app.worker`) with NO HTTP server → it shows `unhealthy` forever despite functioning correctly (consumer starts + drains the resume stream — live-proven). Fix: replace with a consumer-liveness check (heartbeat key/file, or `pgrep -f app.worker`) or drop the healthcheck for the worker block. Non-blocking (does not stop consumption), purely a misleading status. | DONE (heartbeat liveness) | RESOLVED |
| ~~LE-063~~ | 2026-06-02 live-e2e (promote chain) | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-02** — `ProposalsRepo.approve()` (new) walks `proposed → author_reviewing → approved` in one call (each hop DAG- + DB-trigger-legal, H0 unchanged), and the `approve` endpoint now calls it; this restores the documented "proposed/reviewing → approved" contract so a freshly-created proposal can be approved → promoted via the API alone (no DB poke). +1 unit test (`test_approve_from_proposed_walks_through_reviewing`). A distinct FE-facing "begin-review" toggle remains optional. ORIGINAL: **Proposal lifecycle API cannot promote a fresh `proposed` proposal.** The DAG (`app/services/review.py LEGAL_TRANSITIONS`) is `proposed → author_reviewing → approved → promoted`, but the API (`app/api/proposals.py`) exposes only `approve` (sets `approved` directly), `reject`, `edit` (no status change), `promote`, `retract`, `write-back` — there is **no endpoint for `proposed → author_reviewing`**. So `approve` from the initial `proposed` state 409s (`illegal transition proposed → approved`), making `promote` unreachable through the API alone. The 2026-06-02 e2e worked around it with a one-row DB `UPDATE … author_reviewing` (the H0 trigger permits it) before calling the real `approve`+`promote` API. Fix: add a `begin-review` endpoint (`proposed → author_reviewing`), OR have `approve` walk `proposed → author_reviewing → approved` in one step. The FE review flow presumably needs this too. | DONE (approve auto-walks) | RESOLVED |

| ~~D-KNOWLEDGE-ENRICHED-WRITEBACK-IDEMPOTENCY~~ | 2026-06-04 E2 live-smoke | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-04.** (Investigation corrected the original wording: `internal_enrichment.py` was ALREADY a `MERGE`, never a raw CREATE.) The real defect: the anchor MERGEd on `(user_id, glossary_entity_id)` while `ON CREATE SET e.id = canon_id` (a name+kind hash) — so a NEW glossary_entity_id mapping to an existing `canon_id` (glossary entity deleted+recreated same name, or rename) MISSED the node and tripped `UNIQUE(:Entity.id)` → 500. This also mismatched the canonical glossary→KG sync, which MERGEs `(:Entity {id})`. **Fix:** the write-back now MERGEs on the canonical `id` (matching the sync), frees a stale `glossary_entity_id` from any other node first (null-before-claim, mirroring `entities.py`), and ON MATCH only coalesces the anchor (H0 canon untouched). Facts re-key to the anchor by `id`. **Live-proven** (`scripts/smoke_writeback_idempotency.py`, rebuilt knowledge-service): two write-backs with DIFFERENT glossary_entity_ids for the same canonical entity → both 200 + re-promote 200; cypher confirms a SINGLE `:Entity` node (no duplicate id); test nodes cleaned. | DONE | RESOLVED |

| ~~D-COMPOSE-S3-UPLOAD-REAPER~~ | 2026-06-04 Compose slice 3 /review-impl #4 | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-04.** New worker reaper (`app/worker/reaper.py`, wired into `app/worker/__main__.py` alongside the heartbeat, `reaper_enabled`/`reaper_interval_s` config) runs a periodic best-effort sweep: (1) `sweep_stale_uploads` flips `processing` rows older than `upload_stale_processing_s` (default 30m) → `failed` (the files branch stops 409-ing forever); (2) `sweep_orphan_objects` lists the MinIO bucket and deletes objects whose `enrichment_upload` row never landed, but ONLY past `upload_orphan_grace_s` (default 1h) so an in-flight upload is never raced; (3) `sweep_ephemeral_corpora` (see D-COMPOSE-CONTEXT-CORPUS-SCOPE). Each sweep is independent (`reap_once` best-effort) so one failure never aborts the others or crashes the worker. +`minio_client.list_objects` + 9 unit tests (stale/orphan-age/no-op/best-effort). | DONE | RESOLVED |
| ~~D-COMPOSE-S3-OCR-LANG~~ | 2026-06-04 Compose slice 3 /review-impl #5 | ✅ **RESOLVED 2026-06-04.** `extract.py` `tesseract_lang_for(profile.language)` maps a book's language → a Tesseract lang string, restricted to the packs SHIPPED in the image (`_INSTALLED_OCR_LANGS` = chi_sim/chi_tra/eng/jpn/vie — the platform's en/vi/ja/zh-TW locales; Dockerfile updated to install jpn+vie) so a book whose pack isn't installed falls back to the CJK+English superset rather than requesting a missing pack (which fails OCR entirely). `eng` is always folded in (embedded Latin); we do NOT add every pack (slows Tesseract). `uploads._extract_and_store` resolves the lang from the book profile for PDFs only (txt/docx/epub need none) and threads it `extract_text → _extract_pdf → _ocr_pdf_pages`. +7 unit tests (zh/zh-TW/zh-Hans/en/ja/vi/auto/unknown + threading). | DONE | RESOLVED |

Root cause was NOT dep-drift — commit `0bf049cd` switched the LLM `<select>` to send `user_model_id` (`value={m.user_model_id}`) but didn't update these tests, which still drove it with the old `provider_model_name` (`'gpt-5'`, not a valid option) → the select stayed empty → the estimate query never enabled → mocks never called. | 046 | 2026-05-31 session 100 moisture-transport model | **Fixed.** Rewrote `climate.rs::moisture_field` from **averaging** upwind neighbours to **MAX best-path** downwind-directed multi-source transport (`moisture[i] = max over upwind nbrs of moisture[nb] − land_leak − OROGRAPHIC·climb`; windward-edge = off-map upwind ocean). MAX lets moisture reach inland along the wettest route from any upwind sea (multi-directional within the upwind cone) while staying wind-aware (offshore coasts dry, range rain-shadows persist). Result (seed-7 mega): interiors greener, C-group nearly doubled in the full-gradient case (equatorial spread=1: 2.2→3.9 %, `Plain` 55→98), Desert preserved 30.7 % at spread=0; bonus — maritime cooling raised Tundra/Polar (126→375 / 5.7→11 %). Tests: `moisture_takes_wettest_upwind_path` (MAX-vs-AVG discriminator), `rain_shadow_follows_the_wind` preserved. Full lib 399 green. Residual (abundant *literal* Temperate plains) split to optional **#047 (8-zone mapping)**. Commit: (this session). |

Fix: split `ObstaclePlacer` into `ObstacleSourcePlacer` (places Mountain/Lake markers on Open tiles **pre-erosion**, dual-gated per-zone + map-wide) → `RiverPlacer` (carves the wide-open zone) → `ObstacleFillPlacer` (erode + fill the rest post-river, skipping river Water; erosion also skips Water fords). Golden fixture river ford ratio: **~0.77 crossings → 0.06 fords** (AC-2 gates < 0.25). Cost tradeoff accepted: continent 6.46 s → 10.56 s (river now carves a real barrier). Strict dual `would_seal_a_gap` gate unchanged. `/review-impl` caught a HIGH (source placer needed the map-wide gate arm, not per-zone only) — fixed + regression-tested. Spec: [`docs/specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md`](../specs/2026-05-22-tilemap-river-barrier-reorder.md). |

The first sorted candidate that passes IS the winner (spec §4 proof: `first ∈ V` of sorted `S` == `argmax_{v ∈ V} (score, -flat)`). AC-1 oracle: 405 agreements across 3 fixtures × 3 templates × 3 optimisations × 3 min-distances × 5 sequential placements. Border-seal completeness is irrelevant to correctness; the original "border thickness" framing was the wrong lens. |
